page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 24 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores with multithreading enabled. The x86_64 architecture processor operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance (top 25%) for small-block cached read operations, but drop to average (middle 50%) for large-block uncached read and write operations. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags such as AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and AVX-512 extensions. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong multi-core Geekbench scores, high PassMark database operations throughput, and strong Redis SET performance.

For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for small models (135M parameters) in both prompt processing and text generation. However, performance degrades to average for medium models (7B parameters) and becomes weak (bottom 25%) for text generation on large models (70B parameters), highlighting a clear hardware limitation for heavy AI workloads due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and strong integer and cryptographic performance make this instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and web serving, while the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage solutions.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.7 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside strong database and Redis operations. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, and large-scale LLM inference is weak due to the absence of a GPU. This instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and database workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance, suitable for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, offering strong database and multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-tier single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core compute and strong multi-core benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
